steps.title: Keep focus on the curve
steps.content: The sentence begins with a theoretical relationship between tax rates and revenues. The completion should continue that focus by explaining what happened to the curve representing that relationship in 1974.

steps.title: Choose the clear clause
steps.content: Choice A and Choice B shift the subject to Arthur Laffer, making the opening noun phrase awkwardly detached. Choice C keeps the curve as the subject and explains the napkin sketch. Choice D awkwardly focuses on the napkin. The answer is C.
